subject: Chemistry
question: Enthalpy of neutralization of all the strong acids and strong bases has the same value because
option1: Neutralization leads to the formation of salt and \({H_2}O\)
option2: Strong acid and bases are ionic substances
option3: Acids always give ride \({H^ + }\) ions and bases always furnish \(O{H^ - }\) ions
option4: The net chemical change involves the combination of \({H^ + }\) and \(O{H^ - }\) ions to form water
remarks: Correct answer is "The net chemical change involves the combination of \({H^ + }\) and \(O{H^ - }\) ions to form water"

question: It is the amount of heat absorbed or evolved when one mole of the compound is formed from its elements.
option1: Standard enthalpy of atomization of an element
option2: Standard enthalpy of formation of a compound
option4: Standard enthalpy of combustion of a substance
remarks: Correct answer is "Standard enthalpy of formation of a compound"

subject: Chemistry
question: Heat of solution is defined as?
option1: Heat required to dissolve one gm molecules in large excess of water
option2: Heat evolved, when one gm molecule is dissolved in large excess of water
option3: Change in heat content of the system when one gm molecule of the solute so they further dilution of solution causes no heat change
remarks: Correct answer is "Change in heat content of the system when one gm molecule of the solute so they further dilution of solution causes no heat change"
